How They Made It: The Extraordinary Second Season of \xe2\x80\x98Barry\xe2\x80\x99

The Ringer · by Alan Siegel · May 20, 2019

Late in Sunday\xe2\x80\x99s second season finale of Barry, Bill Hader\xe2\x80\x99s homicidal title character experiences a rare moment of relief. He\xe2\x80\x99s just learned that his acting teacher, Gene Cousineau, who that day had been framed for a murder that Barry committed, is being released from jail. The hitman\xe2\x80\x99s mentor, it turns out, is cleared by a bit of clever evidence planting (more on that later).

\xe2\x80\x9cCan you tell him that he\xe2\x80\x99s gonna be OK,\xe2\x80\x9d Barry says to his guru\xe2\x80\x99s son Leo, who\xe2\x80\x99d told him over the phone about the police clearing his father, \xe2\x80\x9cand that he was right, I\xe2\x80\x99m pretty sure that people can change.\xe2\x80\x9d

At the beginning of the call, he identifies himself as Barry Block. But before hanging up, he drops his stage name and says that it\xe2\x80\x99s Barry Berkman. Sitting at a bus stop on a rainy night, he looks straight ahead and then down, bulges his cheek with his tongue, and hangs his head. He\xe2\x80\x99s spent the last eight episodes resisting his urge to kill. His rabbi isn\xe2\x80\x99t going to prison. And though it seems like a terrifying possibility, he avoids physically harming his girlfriend Sally. For a short time at least, it appears that he\xe2\x80\x99s made progress.

\xe2\x80\x9cWe wanted it to feel like the end of the show,\xe2\x80\x9d said executive producer Alec Berg, who created the series with Hader. \xe2\x80\x9cThe idea is that you feel like, \xe2\x80\x98Oh my God, he\xe2\x80\x99s changed. He\xe2\x80\x99s through the woods.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d

\xe2\x80\x9cWe wanted it to feel like the end of the show,\xe2\x80\x9d said executive producer Alec Berg, who created the series with Hader. \xe2\x80\x9cThe idea is that you feel like, \xe2\x80\x98Oh my God, he\xe2\x80\x99s changed. He\xe2\x80\x99s through the woods.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d

At that point, there was initially going to be a shot of two Hollywood agents\xe2\x80\x94who minutes earlier had reluctantly watched the members of Cousineau\xe2\x80\x99s class perform scenes about their own lives\xe2\x80\x94talking. One was supposed to say, \xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s weird that Barry didn\xe2\x80\x99t do a piece. I wanted to know what his truth was.\xe2\x80\x9d The line got cut, Berg said, for being \xe2\x80\x9ckind of too on the nose.\xe2\x80\x9d

Instead, Barry simply receives a text. It\xe2\x80\x99s from his gregarious, Yoshinoya Beef Bowl\xe2\x80\x93loving Chechen crime boss friend NoHo Hank. He informs his pal that he\xe2\x80\x99s with Barry\xe2\x80\x99s slimy handler Fuches, who had vindictively set up Cousineau. As soon as he sees the message, Barry\xe2\x80\x99s relief turns to rage. Here Hader\xe2\x80\x99s eyes open impossibly wide. His expression brought to mind the look that another HBO antihero recently flashed before setting the world on fire.

\xe2\x80\x9cThe minute you say in life, \xe2\x80\x98Oh good, I got that licked, I figured that out,\xe2\x80\x99 you immediately fall back,\xe2\x80\x9d said Hader, who directed the finale, \xe2\x80\x9cberkman/block.\xe2\x80\x9d And oh boy, does Barry fall back. While searching for Fuches, he guns down countless hapless mobsters.

In truth, Barry\xe2\x80\x99s personal growth is an illusion. Efforts to reform himself be damned, this is who he is. As Hader put it: \xe2\x80\x9ca violent piece of shit.\xe2\x80\x9d In life, there\xe2\x80\x99s always the hope that even people like Barry can change. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd then you realize,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said, \xe2\x80\x9cOh, maybe they can\xe2\x80\x99t.\xe2\x80\x9d

The first season of Barry is spent examining whether a contract killer could logistically change careers. After getting the acting bug while out on a hit in Los Angeles, Barry starts to believe that it\xe2\x80\x99s possible. \xe2\x80\x9cFuches has made him a killer and he was kind of unwittingly pushed into this life,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said, \xe2\x80\x9cand he realizes, \xe2\x80\x98Wait, I want to change.\xe2\x80\x99 But everything forcing his way back into a life of being a killer was circumstantial. It\xe2\x80\x99s not really about who he is inside that\xe2\x80\x99s trapping him.\xe2\x80\x9d

But as the first run of episodes progresses, it becomes clear that Barry\xe2\x80\x99s cruel line of work is inescapable. The season ends with him murdering Cousineau\xe2\x80\x99s girlfriend, Detective Janice Moss, because she\xe2\x80\x99s on to him. The cruel twist would\xe2\x80\x99ve made for a fitting series finale. This is who he is.

The second season takes up an even trickier challenge. The show burrows into Barry\xe2\x80\x99s head. In year two, Berg said, Barry begins to question his bloodthirsty nature. \xe2\x80\x9cCan he change that? What\xe2\x80\x99s wrong with him and why did he do those things?\xe2\x80\x9d

Throughout this season, Barry\xe2\x80\x99s characters grappled with how to process trauma. Sometimes, the need to move forward from difficult situations led to a distorted truth. Embellishment became powerful armor.

\xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s easier to go, \xe2\x80\x98God, this is what I wish I would\xe2\x80\x99ve done,\xe2\x80\x99 and make that my story,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. \xe2\x80\x9cI think a lot of times we do that as humans. We change it in our heads and it becomes the real thing. \xe2\x80\x98I told that person off.\xe2\x80\x99 When, in reality, you didn\xe2\x80\x99t. You didn\xe2\x80\x99t tell them off. But you tell yourself that.\xe2\x80\x9d In movies and television, he added, \xe2\x80\x9cThose stories are the ones that mostly get made. Because they\xe2\x80\x99re stories that people like.\xe2\x80\x9d

As Fuches reminds Barry, the real-life William Wallace didn\xe2\x80\x99t really give that iconic speech in Braveheart. People, he said, \xe2\x80\x9cdon\xe2\x80\x99t want honest. They want entertainment.\xe2\x80\x9d

On the surface, Barry and Sally begin the season in similar spots. Desperate to put their painful pasts behind them, both have reinvented themselves. But deep down, they couldn\xe2\x80\x99t be more different. Sally survived an abusive relationship. Barry is a mass murderer. A woman who leaves a violent partner doesn\xe2\x80\x99t need redemption. A killer who believes that he can be redeemed is delusional.

And while Sally\xe2\x80\x99s Hollywood-style careerism is often played for laughs\xe2\x80\x94e.g., when she tells a needy Barry that they \xe2\x80\x9ccan definitely find a window\xe2\x80\x9d to hang out\xe2\x80\x94for her it\xe2\x80\x99s an important coping mechanism. \xe2\x80\x9cThe whole season is watching somebody yo-yo between what really happened in their past and what they\xe2\x80\x99ve worked so hard to hold onto in the present,\xe2\x80\x9d said Sarah Goldberg, who plays Sally. \xe2\x80\x9cFor literal survival.\xe2\x80\x9d Yet like Carmela Soprano and Skyler White before her, Sally has idiotic detractors. \xe2\x80\x9cThere are people who are like, \xe2\x80\x98I don\xe2\x80\x99t know if I want Barry to end up with Sally, I don\xe2\x80\x99t like her,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cOh my God, you don\xe2\x80\x99t want Barry to end up with Sally? Sally\xe2\x80\x99s with a serial killer and she doesn\xe2\x80\x99t know!\xe2\x80\x9d

\xe2\x80\x9cThere are people who are like, \xe2\x80\x98I don\xe2\x80\x99t know if I want Barry to end up with Sally, I don\xe2\x80\x99t like her,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cOh my God, you don\xe2\x80\x99t want Barry to end up with Sally? Sally\xe2\x80\x99s with a serial killer and she doesn\xe2\x80\x99t know!\xe2\x80\x9d

Like Sally, the self-absorbed Cousineau is unaware that Barry is a mercenary. In this year\xe2\x80\x99s premiere, he\xe2\x80\x99s despondent. His girlfriend is missing. (He has no idea that Barry killed her.) The acting coach, played memorably by Henry Winkler as a Jewish grandpa-like Svengali, is reinvigorated when Barry takes the stage in class and tells the story of his first kill as a Marine in Afghanistan. A flashback confirms that it was the exact opposite of a heroic act. Perched 700 yards away, Barry shoots two civilians acting \xe2\x80\x9csuspicious.\xe2\x80\x9d His fellow soldiers, who refer to Barry\xe2\x80\x99s victims as \xe2\x80\x9csheep fuckers,\xe2\x80\x9d wildly cheer their buddy\xe2\x80\x99s prowess as a sniper.

Oblivious to just how heinous Barry\xe2\x80\x99s actions were, Cousineau challenges his students to find and perform their own \xe2\x80\x9csheep fucker moments.\xe2\x80\x9d It takes some coaxing by her teacher, but an initially apprehensive Sally decides to write a scene in which she dramatically stands up to, and leaves, her abusive ex-husband. Through a Skype session with an old friend who refuses to corroborate the details of the incident, Sally reveals herself to be an unreliable narrator.

\xe2\x80\x9cShe has sort of told herself this story again and again and again as gospel,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cThat this is how it went down and that she needs to hold onto that for survival. And slowly we watch that veneer get chipped away. \xe2\x80\xa6 She\xe2\x80\x99s looking for an alibi and her friend won\xe2\x80\x99t participate in the lie.\xe2\x80\x9d

At the end of Episode 3, after hearing that Sally is writing about being the survivor of his abuse, her ex Sam shows up in Los Angeles. Played subtly by Joe Massingill, he eventually asks Sally to meet him in his hotel room. There his faux nice-guy charm disappears. He confronts Sally about her piece, which he begs her not to share with the public. \xe2\x80\x9cEveryone\xe2\x80\x99s the hero of their own story, right?\xe2\x80\x9d he says. \xe2\x80\x9cLying bitch.\xe2\x80\x9d Then, as a gun-toting Barry secretly lurks, Sally bravely walks out.

Midway through our interview, Goldberg paused the conversation. The subject matter that we were chatting about was too weighty for her not to point out the obvious: \xe2\x80\x9cCan you believe we\xe2\x80\x99re talking about a half-hour comedy?\xe2\x80\x9d

Barry is brutal. Barry is also funny. In fact, it blurs the line between comedy and violence better than any show since The Sopranos.

Take the scene in the third episode after Sally\xe2\x80\x99s Skype call. Unbeknownst to her, Hank\xe2\x80\x99s henchman Akhmal has just pumped bullets into Barry\xe2\x80\x99s bedroom. When Sally walks in, there are feathers everywhere. She has no clue what happened. All she can think about is the potentially career-making scene that she\xe2\x80\x99s writing; everything else fades into the background.

\xe2\x80\x9cBill gave me this note where he\xe2\x80\x99s like, \xe2\x80\x98Just take one of the feathers and start playing with the feather from the pillow being shot,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cI was like, \xe2\x80\x98Is this too far? Like, have we gone too far?\xe2\x80\x99 So we shot it and for safety we shot one where I didn\xe2\x80\x99t. But it worked so well. It\xe2\x80\x99s so funny: We could go that far because she has such deep tunnel vision.\xe2\x80\x9d

Barry routinely manages to mix the satisfyingly silly with a bleakness. Every line Anthony Carrigan delivers as Barry\xe2\x80\x99s frenemy Hank is pitched in a comic tone\xe2\x80\x94it\xe2\x80\x99s clear he\xe2\x80\x99s in a comedy. But there\xe2\x80\x99s something grounded happening too. As Kate Knibbs of The Ringer pointed out in an interview with the actor, \xe2\x80\x9cHis Chechen-goes-SoCal accent is so convincing on the show that it is a little jarring to hear his real, standard American English speaking voice.\xe2\x80\x9d

Berg envisions Hank as having learned to speak English in Chechnya while watching old television shows like Three\xe2\x80\x99s Company, Mork & Mindy, and Miami Vice. He loves using American idioms, even if he unwittingly tends to twist them into malapropisms. Like these: \xe2\x80\x9cMassive fumble turnover for Hank.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cI know what Sonny and Cher would say, \xe2\x80\x98That\xe2\x80\x99s on you, babe.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cGet out of free jail.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cI am optometrist by nature.\xe2\x80\x9d

The show\xe2\x80\x99s writers, however, took great care to make Hank ridiculous, but not too ridiculous. \xe2\x80\x9cIf you have two of something, the overall piece is funnier than if you have four of something,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cEven though four is more than two.\xe2\x80\x9d Of Carrigan, Berg said: \xe2\x80\x9cAnthony is a genius at playing that character. \xe2\x80\x98Massive fumble turnover\xe2\x80\x99 was one of his. \xe2\x80\xa6 All of his little improvs and additions and things are just so in the pocket of that character.\xe2\x80\x9d

Barry seesaws between disturbing and hysterical, sometimes in the same scene. After resisting killing Sam in Episode 4, Barry unburdens himself to Cousineau. In Afghanistan\xe2\x80\x99s Korengal Valley, Barry explains in detail, he responded to his friend getting shot by murdering an innocent villager. To Barry\xe2\x80\x99s surprise, Cousineau withholds judgment. \xe2\x80\x9cYou did a terrible thing,\xe2\x80\x9d he says. \xe2\x80\x9cBut do I think that defines you? No.\xe2\x80\x9d

\xe2\x80\x9cThe scene was so heavy and Alec is really good at being like, \xe2\x80\x98We need a joke here,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. Berg had had no doubt what Cousineau would do to close out a heart-to-heart with Barry. He\xe2\x80\x99d charge him for the session. \xe2\x80\x9cIn a conversation about people\xe2\x80\x99s true nature and who they really are!\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cThere\xe2\x80\x99s nothing wrong with breaking this lovely moment to go get his ledger so that he can bill Barry for his time.\xe2\x80\x9d

The next episode, the year\xe2\x80\x99s most outrageously ambitious installment, contained no such lovely moments. Directed by Hader, \xe2\x80\x9cronny/lily\xe2\x80\x9d feels like a 30-minute-long, ultraviolent punch line. The premise is this: Detective Moss\xe2\x80\x99s partner, John Loach, has figured out that Barry killed her. Loach uses Fuches to blackmail Barry into murdering the cop\xe2\x80\x99s estranged wife\xe2\x80\x99s lover, Ronny. But there are issues. First, Barry doesn\xe2\x80\x99t want to kill the guy. Second, the guy is basically unkillable.

After arriving at Ronny\xe2\x80\x99s house, Barry tries to get him to flee. Then, after catching a glimpse of the home\xe2\x80\x99s vast trophy room, he realizes that his target is a taekwondo master. \xe2\x80\x9cBarry\xe2\x80\x99s kind of hoping these aren\xe2\x80\x99t his trophies,\xe2\x80\x9d said Hader, whose character flat-out asks Ronny who the awards belong to. \xe2\x80\x9cThat made our stunt coordinator Wade [Allen] laugh so hard.\xe2\x80\x9d

Played by actor and martial artist Daniel Bernhardt, who\xe2\x80\x99s had parts in John Wick, Logan, and Atomic Blonde, Ronny then kicks Barry in the chest. What follows is an exhausting, over-the-top fight that leaves Barry with a hilariously repulsive goatee of blood. (\xe2\x80\x9cIt was like having Elmer\xe2\x80\x99s Glue on my face,\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said.) In that scene, Hader\xe2\x80\x99s stunt double did much of the heavy lifting. \xe2\x80\x9cI strategically wrote that Barry had a mask on because I was like, \xe2\x80\x98I am not doing this stuff,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said.

The brawl is actually the day\xe2\x80\x99s undercard. When Ronny\xe2\x80\x99s young daughter, Lily, comes home to find her dad incapacitated, she attacks Barry. For the role, Jessie Giacomazzi\xe2\x80\x94whose parents are stunt coordinators and performers\xe2\x80\x94channeled a feral mongoose. Her intense performance was acrobatic enough to inspire a Reddit thread called \xe2\x80\x9cWas that girl real or CGI???\xe2\x80\x9d

The episode, which ends with Ronny and Loach dead, Barry badly wounded, and Lily nowhere to be found, was designed to stand alone. Berg has liked the concept of one-offs since seeing the 1986 Moonlighting episode \xe2\x80\x9cAtomic Shakespeare,\xe2\x80\x9d a spoof of The Taming of the Shrew. \xe2\x80\x9cI remember going, \xe2\x80\x98Oh my God, in the middle of the show they just completely left planet Earth and they did this one stand-alone episode and the next week they were just back to normal,\xe2\x80\x9d he said. \xe2\x80\x9cI remember going, \xe2\x80\x98You can do that?\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d

For Season 1, the creators of Barry considered a self-contained, real-time half hour about Barry botching a hit. \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99s on one side of the wall and the guy he\xe2\x80\x99s trying to kill is on the other and they both have guns,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd they\xe2\x80\x99re just sort of stuck there.\xe2\x80\x9d The show\xe2\x80\x99s staff, however, never quite figured out where the episode would fit.

But this year, \xe2\x80\x9cronny/lily\xe2\x80\x9d worked as a midseason surprise. \xe2\x80\x9cThat was like the halftime show,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. Hader said that while watching the episode he and his editor, Jeff Buchanan, thought that it felt kind of like \xe2\x80\x9cPine Barrens.\xe2\x80\x9d In the Sopranos classic, Christopher and Paulie spend the night in the snowy woods bickering after losing track of a seemingly indestructible Russian. Like him, Lily may be lost forever. Or maybe not. Feral mongooses do have excellent survival skills.

During a meeting in a park at the start of Episode 6, Barry makes a last-ditch attempt to extricate himself from Fuches. Here Stephen Root\xe2\x80\x94whose emotionally manipulative, golf-polo-wearing boomer dad aura is strong\xe2\x80\x94is at his best. After his prot\xc3\xa9g\xc3\xa9 says that they can no longer work together and that Mr. Cousineau truly understands and accepts him, Fuches goes for Barry\xe2\x80\x99s throat.

\xe2\x80\x9cDid you tell him you killed his girlfriend?\xe2\x80\x9d he says. Barry is speechless and mumbles that that never needs to come up. \xe2\x80\x9cFuches is saying, \xe2\x80\x98Of course he\xe2\x80\x99s telling you that, because you didn\xe2\x80\x99t tell him the truth,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cIf you actually disclosed who you were, he would\xe2\x80\x99ve told you something different.\xe2\x80\x9d Fuches tells Barry that he\xe2\x80\x99s in denial. \xe2\x80\x9cYou killed Moss out of your own self-interest,\xe2\x80\x9d he says. \xe2\x80\x9cYou made a choice to kill her. Because you\xe2\x80\x99re a violent guy, Barry. I built a world where that\xe2\x80\x99s an asset.\xe2\x80\x9d He\xe2\x80\x99s cruel, but he\xe2\x80\x99s right. \xe2\x80\x9cFuches\xe2\x80\x99s whole point is, \xe2\x80\x98I didn\xe2\x80\x99t make you this,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9c\xe2\x80\x98You made yourself into this and I accommodated you. And I gave you a place where what you are is good.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d

As Barry stalks off, Fuches yells, \xe2\x80\x9cI guess everyone\xe2\x80\x99s a hero of their own story, right?\xe2\x80\x9d It\xe2\x80\x99s almost exactly what Sam had shouted at Sally. Both Barry and Sally, it turns out, are dealing with the damage that an abuser has inflicted. But their circumstances are much different. \xe2\x80\x9cShe can actually move toward something more honest, but for Barry that would mean jail,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99s really trapped.\xe2\x80\x9d

By the end of the season, Sally has decided to reshape her piece into a rawer, truer story. In the scene, Sam, played by Barry, is supposed to choke Sally. At first, Barry struggles to get to a place where he even can pretend to hurt her.

Pre-rehearsal advice from Cousineau\xe2\x80\x94\xe2\x80\x9cYou don\xe2\x80\x99t have to literally tell your story in order to use your story\xe2\x80\x9d\xe2\x80\x94is what Barry needs. The acting teacher assumes that an apprehensive Barry will channel the destructive rage he felt in Korengal. What he\xe2\x80\x99ll never know is where else Barry will go.

Originally, Cousineau guided Barry through past experiences in order to drum up anger. \xe2\x80\x9cLike, \xe2\x80\x98When was a time when you felt angry? When was a time you felt betrayed?\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd Alec and I wrote it and shot it and when we edited it it just felt wrong. It was too long and it just didn\xe2\x80\x99t work. And we were like, \xe2\x80\x98It shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t be about all the stuff we don\xe2\x80\x99t know. It should be about a thing that we do know about.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Like the murder of Moss. So as Barry gears up to play the scene, he closes his eyes. Images of Korengal enter his head. And so does the memory of shooting Janice. The resulting performance is Barry\xe2\x80\x99s best. It also serves to remind the audience of who he truly is.

Sally\xe2\x80\x99s agent Lindsay is impressed by the piece and soon the actress is in a conference room sitting across from a powerful TV producer. But what he tries to sell her is called Payback Ladies, a series about women who team up to kill their abusive husbands. Hader said that the idea came from Episode 7 writer Liz Sarnoff, who wanted to skewer the retrograde revenge fantasy genre. Sally turns down the role.

\xe2\x80\x9cThe thing that she\xe2\x80\x99s been chasing is right in front of her and she decides because she has morals and because she has a belief system that she\xe2\x80\x99s not gonna take this cheap route,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cWhich I think is this very self-sacrificial, brave act.\xe2\x80\x9d

Later, in the middle of helping Barry practice his lines for a movie audition that he lands when one of her agents notices that he\xe2\x80\x99s the ideal height\xe2\x80\x946-foot-2\xe2\x80\x94for a role, Sally stops him and unloads a two-and-a-half-minute monologue. It covers everything from her fear of sharing her story with the world, the entertainment industry\xe2\x80\x99s treatment of women, to the jealousy she\xe2\x80\x99s feeling. Goldberg\xe2\x80\x99s breathless, pitch-perfect delivery makes the speech a season highlight.

And as for Barry\xe2\x80\x99s audition \xe2\x80\xa6

It\xe2\x80\x99s for a comedy called Swim Instructors. Director Jay Roach and casting director Allison Jones make cameos as versions of themselves. (\xe2\x80\x9cIt was so weird that it\xe2\x80\x99s somebody who\xe2\x80\x99s, like, the gatekeeper of so many comedic actors\xe2\x80\x99 lives and it\xe2\x80\x99s us directing her,\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said of Jones.) Panicked after learning that a payback-seeking Fuches has lured Cousineau to Moss\xe2\x80\x99s body, Barry reads his lines distractedly and walks out. Incredulous, Roach turns to Jones and says, \xe2\x80\x9cHe did not give a fuck.\xe2\x80\x9d But follows with, \xe2\x80\x9cWhat\xe2\x80\x99s his name again?\xe2\x80\x9d Jones, who\xe2\x80\x99s spent the last two decades working with legendary improvisers, responds with an improvised line of her own: \xe2\x80\x9cBarry Block. ier had reluctantly watched the members of Cousineau\xe2\x80\x99s class perform scenes about their own lives\xe2\x80\x94 talking. One was supposed to say, \xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s weird that Barry didn\xe2\x80\x99t do a piece. I wanted to know what his truth was.\xe2\x80\x9d The line got cut, Berg said, for being \xe2\x80\x9ckind of too on the nose.\xe2\x80\x9d Instead, Barry simply receives a text. It\xe2\x80\x99s from his gregarious, Yoshinoya Beef Bowl\xe2\x80\x93 loving Chechen crime boss friend NoHo Hank. He informs his pal that he\xe2\x80\x99s with Barry\xe2\x80\x99s slimy handler Fuches, who had vindictively set up Cousineau. As soon as he sees the message, Barry\xe2\x80\x99s relief turns to rage. Here Hader\xe2\x80\x99s eyes open impossibly wide. His expression brought to mind the look that another HBO antihero recently flashed before setting the world on fire. Bill Hader and Alec Berg Patrick McMullan via Getty Image \xe2\x80\x9cThe minute you say in life, \xe2\x80\x98Oh good, I got that licked, I figured that out,\xe2\x80\x99 you immediately fall back,\xe2\x80\x9d said Hader, who directed the finale, \xe2\x80\x9cberkman block.\xe2\x80\x9d And oh boy, does Barry fall back. While searching for Fuches, he guns down countless hapless mobsters. In truth, Barry\xe2\x80\x99s personal growth is an illusion. Efforts to reform himself be damned, this is who he is. As Hader put it: \xe2\x80\x9ca violent piece of shit.\xe2\x80\x9d In life, there\xe2\x80\x99s always the hope that even people like Barry can change. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd then you realize,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said, \xe2\x80\x9c Oh, maybe they can\xe2\x80\x99t. \xe2\x80\x9d The first season of Barry is spent examining whether a contract killer could logistically change careers. After getting the acting bug while out on a hit in Los Angeles, Barry starts to believe that it\xe2\x80\x99s possible. \xe2\x80\x9cFuches has made him a killer and he was kind of unwittingly pushed into this life,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said, \xe2\x80\x9cand he realizes, \xe2\x80\x98Wait, I want to change.\xe2\x80\x99 But everything forcing his way back into a life of being a killer was circumstantial. It\xe2\x80\x99s not really about who he is inside that\xe2\x80\x99s trapping him.\xe2\x80\x9d But as the first run of episodes progresses, it becomes clear that Barry\xe2\x80\x99s cruel line of work is inescapable. The season ends with him murdering Cousineau\xe2\x80\x99s girlfriend, Detective Janice Moss, because she\xe2\x80\x99s on to him. The cruel twist would\xe2\x80\x99ve made for a fitting series finale. This is who he is. The second season takes up an even trickier challenge. The show burrows into Barry\xe2\x80\x99s head. In year two, Berg said, Barry begins to question his bloodthirsty nature. \xe2\x80\x9cCan he change that? What\xe2\x80\x99s wrong with him and why did he do those things?\xe2\x80\x9d Throughout this season, Barry \xe2\x80\x99s characters grappled with how to process trauma. Sometimes, the need to move forward from difficult situations led to a distorted truth. Embellishment became powerful armor. \xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s easier to go, \xe2\x80\x98God, this is what I wish I would\xe2\x80\x99ve done,\xe2\x80\x99 and make that my story,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. \xe2\x80\x9cI think a lot of times we do that as humans. We change it in our heads and it becomes the real thing. \xe2\x80\x98I told that person off.\xe2\x80\x99 When, in reality, you didn\xe2\x80\x99t. You didn\xe2\x80\x99t tell them off. But you tell yourself that.\xe2\x80\x9d In movies and television, he added, \xe2\x80\x9cThose stories are the ones that mostly get made. Because they\xe2\x80\x99re stories that people like.\xe2\x80\x9d As Fuches reminds Barry, the real- life William Wallace didn\xe2\x80\x99t really give that iconic speech in Braveheart . People, he said, \xe2\x80\x9cdon\xe2\x80\x99t want honest. They want entertainment.\xe2\x80\x9d On the surface, Barry and Sally begin the season in similar spots. Desperate to put their painful pasts behind them, both have reinvented themselves. But deep down, they couldn\xe2\x80\x99t be more different. Sally survived an abusive relationship. Barry is a mass murderer. A woman who leaves a violent partner doesn\xe2\x80\x99t need redemption. A killer who believes that he can be redeemed is delusional. And while Sally\xe2\x80\x99s Hollywood- style careerism is often played for laughs\xe2\x80\x94 e.g., when she tells a needy Barry that they \xe2\x80\x9ccan definitely find a window\xe2\x80\x9d to hang out\xe2\x80\x94 for her it\xe2\x80\x99s an important coping mechanism. \xe2\x80\x9cThe whole season is watching somebody yo- yo between what really happened in their past and what they\xe2\x80\x99ve worked so hard to hold onto in the present,\xe2\x80\x9d said Sarah Goldberg, who plays Sally. \xe2\x80\x9cFor literal survival.\xe2\x80\x9d Yet like Carmela Soprano and Skyler White before her, Sally has idiotic detractors. \xe2\x80\x9cThere are people who are like, \xe2\x80\x98I don\xe2\x80\x99t know if I want Barry to end up with Sally, I don\xe2\x80\x99t like her,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cOh my God, you don\xe2\x80\x99t want Barry to end up with Sally? Sally\xe2\x80\x99s with a serial killer and she doesn\xe2\x80\x99t know!\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cThere are people who are like, \xe2\x80\x98I don\xe2\x80\x99t know if I want Barry to end up with Sally, I don\xe2\x80\x99t like her,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cOh my God, you don\xe2\x80\x99t want Barry to end up with Sally? Sally\xe2\x80\x99s with a serial killer and she doesn\xe2\x80\x99t know!\xe2\x80\x9d Like Sally, the self- absorbed Cousineau is unaware that Barry is a mercenary. In this year\xe2\x80\x99s premiere, he\xe2\x80\x99s despondent. His girlfriend is missing. (He has no idea that Barry killed her.) The acting coach, played memorably by Henry Winkler as a Jewish grandpa- like Svengali, is reinvigorated when Barry takes the stage in class and tells the story of his first kill as a Marine in Afghanistan. A flashback confirms that it was the exact opposite of a heroic act. Perched 700 yards away, Barry shoots two civilians acting \xe2\x80\x9csuspicious.\xe2\x80\x9d His fellow soldiers, who refer to Barry\xe2\x80\x99s victims as \xe2\x80\x9csheep fuckers,\xe2\x80\x9d wildly cheer their buddy\xe2\x80\x99s prowess as a sniper. Oblivious to just how heinous Barry\xe2\x80\x99s actions were, Cousineau challenges his students to find and perform their own \xe2\x80\x9csheep fucker moments.\xe2\x80\x9d It takes some coaxing by her teacher, but an initially apprehensive Sally decides to write a scene in which she dramatically stands up to, and leaves, her abusive ex- husband. Through a Skype session with an old friend who refuses to corroborate the details of the incident, Sally reveals herself to be an unreliable narrator. \xe2\x80\x9cShe has sort of told herself this story again and again and again as gospel,\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cThat this is how it went down and that she needs to hold onto that for survival. And slowly we watch that veneer get chipped away. \xe2\x80\xa6 She\xe2\x80\x99s looking for an alibi and her friend won\xe2\x80\x99t participate in the lie.\xe2\x80\x9d At the end of Episode 3, after hearing that Sally is writing about being the survivor of his abuse, her ex Sam shows up in Los Angeles. Played subtly by Joe Massingill, he eventually asks Sally to meet him in his hotel room. There his faux nice- guy charm disappears. He confronts Sally about her piece, which he begs her not to share with the public. \xe2\x80\x9cEveryone\xe2\x80\x99s the hero of their own story, right?\xe2\x80\x9d he says. \xe2\x80\x9cLying bitch.\xe2\x80\x9d Then, as a gun- toting Barry secretly lurks, Sally bravely walks out. Midway through our interview, Goldberg paused the conversation. The subject matter that we were chatting about was too weighty for her not to point out the obvious: \xe2\x80\x9cCan you believe we\xe2\x80\x99re talking about a half- hour comedy?\xe2\x80\x9d Barry is brutal. Barry is also funny. In fact, it blurs the line between comedy and violence better than any show since The Sopranos . Take the scene in the third episode after Sally\xe2\x80\x99s Skype call. Unbeknownst to her, Hank\xe2\x80\x99s henchman Akhmal has just pumped bullets into Barry\xe2\x80\x99s bedroom. When Sally walks in, there are feathers everywhere. She has no clue what happened. All she can think about is the potentially career- making scene that she\xe2\x80\x99s writing; everything else fades into the background. \xe2\x80\x9cBill gave me this note where he\xe2\x80\x99s like, \xe2\x80\x98Just take one of the feathers and start playing with the feather from the pillow being shot,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Goldberg said. \xe2\x80\x9cI was like, \xe2\x80\x98Is this too far? Like, have we gone too far?\xe2\x80\x99 So we shot it and for safety we shot one where I didn\xe2\x80\x99t. But it worked so well. It\xe2\x80\x99s so funny: We could go that far because she has such deep tunnel vision.\xe2\x80\x9d Barry routinely manages to mix the satisfyingly silly with a bleakness. Every line Anthony Carrigan delivers as Barry\xe2\x80\x99s frenemy Hank is pitched in a comic tone\xe2\x80\x94 it\xe2\x80\x99s clear he\xe2\x80\x99s in a comedy. But there\xe2\x80\x99s something grounded happening too. As Kate Knibbs of The Ringer pointed out in an interview with the actor , \xe2\x80\x9cHis Chechen- goes- SoCal accent is so convincing on the show that it is a little jarring to hear his real, standard American English speaking voice.\xe2\x80\x9d Berg envisions Hank as having learned to speak English in Chechnya while watching old television shows like Three\xe2\x80\x99s Company , Mork & Mindy , and Miami Vice . He loves using American idioms, even if he unwittingly tends to twist them into malapropisms. Like these: \xe2\x80\x9cMassive fumble turnover for Hank.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cI know what Sonny and Cher would say, \xe2\x80\x98That\xe2\x80\x99s on you, babe.\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cGet out of free jail.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cI am optometrist by nature.\xe2\x80\x9d The show\xe2\x80\x99s writers, however, took great care to make Hank ridiculous, but not too ridiculous. \xe2\x80\x9cIf you have two of something, the overall piece is funnier than if you have four of something,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cEven though four is more than two.\xe2\x80\x9d Of Carrigan, Berg said: \xe2\x80\x9cAnthony is a genius at playing that character. \xe2\x80\x98Massive fumble turnover\xe2\x80\x99 was one of his. \xe2\x80\xa6 All of his little improvs and additions and things are just so in the pocket of that character.\xe2\x80\x9d Alec Berg and Bill Hader Getty Images for WGAw Barry seesaws between disturbing and hysterical, sometimes in the same scene. After resisting killing Sam in Episode 4, Barry unburdens himself to Cousineau. In Afghanistan\xe2\x80\x99s Korengal Valley, Barry explains in detail, he responded to his friend getting shot by murdering an innocent villager. To Barry\xe2\x80\x99s surprise, Cousineau withholds judgment. \xe2\x80\x9cYou did a terrible thing,\xe2\x80\x9d he says. \xe2\x80\x9cBut do I think that defines you? No.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x9cThe scene was so heavy and Alec is really good at being like, \xe2\x80\x98We need a joke here,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. Berg had had no doubt what Cousineau would do to close out a heart- to- heart with Barry. He\xe2\x80\x99d charge him for the session. \xe2\x80\x9cIn a conversation about people\xe2\x80\x99s true nature and who they really are!\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cThere\xe2\x80\x99s nothing wrong with breaking this lovely moment to go get his ledger so that he can bill Barry for his time.\xe2\x80\x9d The next episode, the year\xe2\x80\x99s most outrageously ambitious installment, contained no such lovely moments. Directed by Hader, \xe2\x80\x9cronny lily\xe2\x80\x9d feels like a 30- minute- long, ultraviolent punch line. The premise is this: Detective Moss\xe2\x80\x99s partner, John Loach, has figured out that Barry killed her. Loach uses Fuches to blackmail Barry into murdering the cop\xe2\x80\x99s estranged wife\xe2\x80\x99s lover, Ronny. But there are issues. First, Barry doesn\xe2\x80\x99t want to kill the guy. Second, the guy is basically unkillable. After arriving at Ronny\xe2\x80\x99s house, Barry tries to get him to flee. Then, after catching a glimpse of the home\xe2\x80\x99s vast trophy room, he realizes that his target is a taekwondo master. \xe2\x80\x9cBarry\xe2\x80\x99s kind of hoping these aren\xe2\x80\x99t his trophies,\xe2\x80\x9d said Hader, whose character flat- out asks Ronny who the awards belong to. \xe2\x80\x9cThat made our stunt coordinator Wade [Allen] laugh so hard.\xe2\x80\x9d Played by actor and martial artist Daniel Bernhardt , who\xe2\x80\x99s had parts in John Wick , Logan , and Atomic Blonde , Ronny then kicks Barry in the chest. What follows is an exhausting, over- the- top fight that leaves Barry with a hilariously repulsive goatee of blood. (\xe2\x80\x9cIt was like having Elmer\xe2\x80\x99s Glue on my face,\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said.) In that scene, Hader\xe2\x80\x99s stunt double did much of the heavy lifting. \xe2\x80\x9cI strategically wrote that Barry had a mask on because I was like, \xe2\x80\x98I am not doing this stuff,\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d Hader said. The brawl is actually the day\xe2\x80\x99s undercard. When Ronny\xe2\x80\x99s young daughter, Lily, comes home to find her dad incapacitated, she attacks Barry. For the role, Jessie Giacomazzi whose parents are stunt coordinators and performers\xe2\x80\x94 channeled a feral mongoose . Her intense performance was acrobatic enough to inspire a Reddit thread called \xe2\x80\x9cWas that girl real or CGI???\xe2\x80\x9d The episode, which ends with Ronny and Loach dead, Barry badly wounded, and Lily nowhere to be found, was designed to stand alone. Berg has liked the concept of one- offs since seeing the 1986 Moonlighting episode \xe2\x80\x9cAtomic Shakespeare,\xe2\x80\x9d a spoof of The Taming of the Shrew . \xe2\x80\x9cI remember going, \xe2\x80\x98Oh my God, in the middle of the show they just completely left planet Earth and they did this one stand- alone episode and the next week they were just back to normal,\xe2\x80\x9d he said. \xe2\x80\x9cI remember going, \xe2\x80\x98You can do that?\xe2\x80\x99\xe2\x80\x9d For Season 1, the creators of Barry considered a self- contained, real- time half hour about Barry botching a hit. \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99s on one side of the wall and the guy he\xe2\x80\x99s trying to kill is on the other and they both have guns,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd they\xe2\x80\x99re just sort of stuck there.\xe2\x80\x9d The show\xe2\x80\x99s staff, however, never quite figured out where the episode would fit. But this year, \xe2\x80\x9cronny lily\xe2\x80\x9d worked as a midseason surprise. \xe2\x80\x9cThat was like the halftime show,\xe2\x80\x9d Berg said. Hader said that while watching the episode he and his editor, Jeff Buchanan, thought that it felt kind of like \xe2\x80\x9cPine Barrens.\xe2\x80\x9d In the Sopranos classic, Christopher and Paulie spend the night in the snowy woods bickering after losing track of a seemingly indestructible Russian. Like him, Lily may be lost forever. Or maybe not. Feral mongooses do have excellent survival skills. During a meeting in a park at the start of Episode 6, Barry makes a last- ditch attempt to extricate himself from Fuches. Here Stephen Root\xe2\x80\x94 whose emotionally manipulative, golf- polo- wearing boomer dad aura is strong\xe2\x80\x94 is at his best. After his prot\xc3\xa9g\xc3\xa9 says that they can no longer work together and that Mr. Cousineau truly understands and accepts him, Fuches goes for Barry\xe2\x80\x99s throat. \xe2\x80\x9cDid you tell him you killed his girlfriend?\xe2\x80\x9d he says. Barry is speechless and mum
